sprint: Cloud-Sync Companion Charm — Studio's Own Traffic Light, Told Through the Companion
task: a direct question -- "why i dont have red, orange, green in studio. everything is on cloud now correct?" -- pointed at World Builder's own signal-dot cloud-sync indicator, which Studio had no equivalent of at all. The user's own framing -- "i would love to have them but in studio style. something which kid might love. we might even use companion for this" -- was taken as full creative latitude. A small, meaning-agnostic .companion-cloud-charm (a floating cloud-emoji charm) was added to js/companionEngine.js's widget, with a plain setSyncBadge(state)/getSyncBadge() pair that only toggles a data-sync-badge attribute -- mirroring the codebase's own established setRichness/boostGlow "generic capability + Director assigns meaning" pattern. js/creatorProjectCache.js gained an onSyncStateChange(id,outcome) pub/sub (mirroring World Builder's projectCache.js sibling), fired from all four real _attemptSync() outcomes; js/projectManager.js's _scheduleCloudProjectSync notifies CompanionDirector the instant a sync is queued. js/companionDirector.js -- the one file allowed to know what any of this means -- subscribes and assigns real meaning: busy (a gentle pulse) while queued, good (a satisfied settle + a glow boost) once genuinely synced, attention (a brief, non-scary wiggle, never a red X) on a real conflict/failure -- gated to the currently-open project and Creator mode only, so a Traveller (no claimed Magic Card) never sees it. Verified via a new Playwright suite exercising the full busy->good, forced-failure->attention, and automatic-recovery-to-good cycles against a real (mocked) versioned Supabase table, plus a full regression pass across the existing Companion/Magic-Card/Cloud-Sync Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites -- every pre-existing failure hit along the way was independently reconfirmed via git stash to already exist on the unmodified codebase, unrelated to this feature. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account.
